author: Arnon-Boneh-Fenzi
title: Proximity Prize MCA benchmark regimes
summary: Table 1 in Open Problems in List Decoding and Correlated Agreement separates the MCA landscape into zero-radius, unique-decoding, Johnson-radius, and capacity-edge regimes.
impact: The current RS-MCA frontier is in the capacity-edge lower-bound regime, not the Johnson-radius positive upper-bound regime.

author: Codex
title: Strict264 quotient-floor proof record
summary: The strict264 quotient-core construction uses the map x -> x^8, an augmented-code list of size binom(64,33), and deep-point conversion to produce at least nine support-wise bad slopes at agreement 264.
impact: This remains a quotient-core mechanism record. The moving-root tangent floor now gives stronger lower bounds at agreement 264, so 265 is not the threshold-pinning target.

author: Codex
title: Strict352 dyadic quotient-core proof record
summary: The strict352 dyadic quotient-core construction proves LD_sw(C,a) >= 7 for every agreement 264 <= a <= 352, and LD_sw(C,352) >= 16 for C = RS[F_17^32,H,256].
impact: This was the quotient-core frontier before the tangent staircase was isolated. It remains a useful mechanism record, but agreement 353 is solved by the generic tangent floor.

author: GPT-5.5 Pro / Codex audit
title: Tangent-star barrier closes the finite-slope non-tangent branch
summary: The moving-root tangent floor is exact in the high-agreement range, and the tangent-star refinement classifies extremizers there.
impact: For RS[F_17^32,H,256], |H|=512, this gives LD_sw(C,506)=7 and LD_sw(C,507)=6 and rules out any seventh finite-slope support-wise MCA branch at agreement a>=507. Remaining checks are projective-slope, CA, curve-MCA, interleaved-list, challenge-field, and protocol ledgers.

author: Scott Hughes
title: L1 d=2 cubic subgroup twisted bound
summary: PR #121 proves a proper-subgroup H^{2k} cubic collision estimate using Fourier reconstruction, multiplicative-character expansion of 1_H, and one-variable Gauss/Kummer-Artin-Schreier input.
impact: This is useful L1 infrastructure for generated-field local limits, but it is not a new MCA leaderboard row or a general higher-moment theorem.

author: Scott Hughes
title: L1 odd-moment Hooley-Katz audit
summary: PR #120 proves the full-affine projective odd-moment collision geometry and audits the Hooley-Katz/Ghorpade-Lachaud constant route.
impact: The audit keeps full-affine, full-torus, and proper-subgroup ledgers separate and shows why the generic full-affine point-count path is not yet enough for the subgroup L1 reserve-scale problem.

author: AllenGrahamHart
title: F1 syndrome-pencil normal form
summary: PR #118 gives a Hankel-pencil incidence test for support-wise line explanations and a matching noncontainment criterion.
impact: This replaces vague extension-line language with an exact locator equation over the actual line field. It is theory infrastructure, not a completed F1 theorem.

author: AllenGrahamHart
title: L2 interleaved codegree reduction
summary: PR #108 proves a two-regime and recursive reduction from interleaved lists to base-code lists at agreements a and 2a-k.
impact: The reduction is unconditional; the desired L2 exponent saving still needs an L1-family tail-list bound.

author: AllenGrahamHart
title: Paper D MCA cap gets a CS25-free route
summary: PR #115 isolates a simple-pole/deep-point averaging proof route for the headline MCA cap under Paper D's finite-field hypotheses.
impact: The MCA cap no longer has to load-bear on the CS25 import, though CA/list statements still need the external import audit.

author: AllenGrahamHart
title: Seven-slope arithmetic gate for strict264
summary: PR #110 confirmed the arithmetic gate: over F_17^32, seven retained slopes at agreement 264 clear 2^-128. The newer quotient-floor proof supplies at least nine slopes.
impact: Kept as the arithmetic gate, but no longer the frontier status. The strict264 row is now a proof record.

author: Scott Hughes
title: Large-domain mixed-Weil energy floor
summary: PR #119 adds a possible L1 route cut for large-domain prefix-dual energy, kept conditional pending the mixed Weil citation and constant check.
impact: Useful as an audit target for L1; not promoted as a theorem and not included as a frontier estimate.

title: Cycle119/Cycle120 ABF-row obstruction
summary: The numerator did not increase: the active value remains 52,747,567,092 bad challenges over F_17^32. The useful update is endpoint precision: Cycle116 reaches the printed ABF closed threshold at delta = 125/256, while Cycle119 gives the strict endpoint delta <= 249/512 if the finite inputs check out.
impact: Keeps the strongest current prize-facing obstruction focused on one smooth rate-1/2 row, without claiming ordinary list decoding, protocol soundness, or an official prize solve.

title: Standalone Cycle120 LDsw proof note
summary: PR #105 adds a compact standalone note for the source-scoped lower bound LD_sw(RS[F_17^32,H,256],262) >= 52,747,567,092.
impact: This is the human-review target that replaces a large raw generated packet for public communication.

title: Exact MCA to line-decoding normalization
summary: PR #102 records the support-wise identity epsilon_mca(C,delta) = LD_sw(C,ceil((1-delta)n))/|F| for the two-source object.
impact: Future updates should use this bridge to avoid denominator and endpoint drift.

title: Extension-line lower floors
summary: PR #103 strengthens the F1 warning: fixed-rate extension-line families have floors on the order of (1-rho)^2/2 for sigma one and (1-rho)^(sigma+1)/(sigma+1)! at fixed sigma before extension-degree dilution.
impact: Naive base-to-extension transfer remains false without an explicit theorem.

title: M1 beta-pushforward spectral audit
summary: PR #104 scans beta-pushforward rows and finds no hidden growing p^2 mass in the tested windows.
impact: This supports the current M1 conductor-style proof target but does not prove the missing bounded-conductor estimate.

title: L1 quotient-budgeted locator route
summary: PRs #99 and #106 add characteristic-zero prefix reductions, bad-prime proof-record machinery, quotient-budgeted locator conjectures, and a full-list quotient proof program.
impact: This is the main positive-route infrastructure for generated-field locator local limits.

title: Sharp interleaved-list target
summary: PR #107 sharpens L2: interleaved-list analysis should charge quotient-core mass diagonally and avoid the naive Cartesian-product numerator.
impact: This turns a vague interleaving hazard into a finite set of support/codegree targets.

title: X1 bridges and Lean formalization stub
summary: PR #101 adds deep-point/interleaved bridge notes, quotient-reduction notes, prize-path maps, and a small Lean scaffold.
impact: This gives future agents a cleaner path from experimental notes toward mechanized statements.
